prompt.content:
│ │ # Storybook Setup │ ## Project Info │ | Property | Value | │ |----------|-------| │ | Version | 10.4.0-alpha.10 | │ | Renderer | @storybook/react | │ | Framework | @storybook/react-vite | │ | Builder | @storybook/builder-vite | │ | Config Dir | `.storybook` | │ | CSF Format | CSF Factory | │ | Addons | @storybook/addon-onboarding, @storybook/addon-themes, │ @storybook/addon-docs, @storybook/addon-designs, │ @storybook/addon-vitest, @storybook/addon-a11y, │ storybook-addon-pseudo-states, @chromatic-com/storybook | │ Attention: The following instructions must be followed in order to │ successfully set up Storybook in this project. Do not skip steps or │ attempt to do them out of order. │ Your goal is to make Storybook fully functional in this project by │ analyzing the codebase, │ configuring the preview with the right decorators, and writing stories │ for some components. │ The end state should be a Storybook where any component — from a small │ button to a full page — can be added without story-specific │ workarounds. All necessary providers, CSS, browser state, and network │ mocks should live in the shared preview so that just rendering the │ component in the story is enough. │ After each created story, run Vitest to verify it renders. │ If the test fails, read the error, fix the issue, and re-run until it │ passes before moving on. │ - Copy real patterns from the codebase │ - Keep the app code unchanged │ - Put the default setup in `.storybook/preview.tsx` │ - Keep app mocking and runtime setup in `.storybook/preview.tsx`, not │ in the stories │ ### Storybook Documentation Reference │ Use the following references to look up Storybook APIs, concepts, or │ examples: │ - Full docs index: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/llms.txthttps://storybook.js.org/llms.txt]8;; │ - See code snippets only with codeOnly=true param e.g. │ ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=ts&codeOnly=truehttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=ts&codeOnly=true]8;; │ Key documentation pages for this task: │ - Writing stories: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories...]8;; │ - Decorators: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/decorators.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/deco...]8;; │ - Args: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/args.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/args.md?re...]8;; │ - Play functions: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/play-function.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-stories/...]8;; │ - Vitest integration: ]8;;https://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-tests/vitest-plugin.md?renderer=react&language=tshttps://storybook.js.org/docs/10/writing-test...]8;; │ Fetch these URLs directly when you need guidance on Storybook APIs or │ patterns. │ ### Step 1: Analyze the codebase │ Read enough of the app to understand the full runtime environment │ before writing any stories. │ Do not stop at `main.tsx` or `App.tsx`. │ Follow imports into providers, pages, hooks, and shared components │ until you know: │ - which providers exist │ - which CSS files are injected │ - which queries fetch data │ - which browser-state reads happen │ - which portals and portal roots exist │ - which pages and components show the real usage patterns │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // src/main.tsx │ import "./index.css"; │ import App from "./App"; │ import { SessionProvider } from "./contexts/SessionContext"; │ createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render( │ │ │ , │ ); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should copy: │ - the `index.css` import │ - the `SessionProvider` │ - the same provider order │ Example of tracing the app deeper: │ ```tsx │ // src/App.tsx │ function App() { │ const { products, loadMoreProducts } = useProducts(); │ const { currentUser, signOut } = useSession(); │ // ... │ } │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useProducts.ts │ const response = await fetch(apiBaseUrl + "/products?page=1"); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // src/hooks/useTheme.ts │ const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem("theme"); │ ``` │ That means the default Storybook setup should discover and prepare: │ - provider state │ - MSW handlers for queries │ - browser-state values that are actually read during render │ ### Step 2: Build one default app environment in preview │ Set up Storybook once so most stories work without story-specific │ setup. │ Start with the smallest faithful environment: │ - the real provider tree │ - the real root CSS │ - seeded browser state if the app reads it during render │ - MSW for network/data queries │ It is fine to seed browser state such as `localStorage`, │ `sessionStorage`, and cookies when the app reads them during render. │ Seed only the specific app-owned keys and values you need. │ Do not clear all `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, or cookies, and do │ not reset Storybook's own state. │ Do not mock or redefine the browser runtime itself. │ The stories run in Vitest browser mode, so the real browser │ environment should already exist. │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import '../src/index.css'; // import global styles │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ import { definePreview } from 'storybook/preview'; │ import { SessionProvider } from '../src/contexts/SessionContext'; │ export default definePreview({ │ decorators: [ │ (Story) => ( │ │ │ │ ), │ ], │ async beforeEach() { │ localStorage.setItem('theme', 'dark'); │ localStorage.setItem('sidebar:open', 'true'); │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ Use this same idea for: │ - providers │ - root CSS │ - browser state │ - dates, and if the app logic depends on them during render then │ always use `mockdate` │ Example with the `mockdate` package: │ ```tsx │ import MockDate from 'mockdate'; │ import { definePreview } from 'storybook/preview'; │ export default definePreview({ │ async beforeEach() { │ MockDate.set('2024-04-01T12:00:00Z'); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ ### Step 3: Support portals with preview-body.html │ If the app uses portals, copy that setup into Storybook too. │ Look for patterns like: │ - `createPortal(...)` │ - modal, dialog, drawer, popover, tooltip, toast, or dropdown portal │ components │ - hard-coded roots such as `#portal-root`, `#modal-root`, │ `#drawer-root`, or `#toast-root` │ Example of what to copy: │ ```tsx │ // real component │ return createPortal(, │ document.getElementById("portal-root")!); │ ``` │ That means Storybook should create the same portal root in │ `.storybook/preview-body.html`: │ ```html │ │
│ ``` │ If the app uses multiple portal roots, create all of them there: │ ```html │ │
│
│
│ ``` │ If a library portals directly to `document.body`, do not add extra │ roots for it. │ Make sure the copied page shell, CSS, and layout still allow overlays, │ fixed positioning, and z-index stacking to render correctly. │ ### Step 4: Mock side effects globally │ All network/data queries should be handled by the default Storybook │ environment. │ - Always use `msw-storybook-addon` for query mocking. │ - If you introduce MSW, run `npx msw init ./public --save` to create │ the worker file. │ - Make sure Storybook serves `./public` as a static dir so │ `mockServiceWorker.js` is available. │ - Do not mock `fetch` directly. │ - Network/data queries should return deterministic mock data. │ - If you need to change dependencies, first check the lockfile and use │ that package manager for the change. │ Example of copying a real fetch pattern into shared handlers: │ ```ts │ // real app hook │ const response = await fetch( │ apiBaseUrl + │ "/products?" + │ new URLSearchParams({ │ page: "1", │ sort: "featured", │ }), │ ); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // .storybook/msw-handlers.ts │ import { http, HttpResponse } from "msw"; │ export const mswHandlers = { │ products: [ │ http.get("]8;;https://api.example.com/products",https://api.example.com/products",]8;; │ () => │ HttpResponse.json({ │ items: [ │ { │ id: "product-1", │ name: "Example product", │ description: "Mock product description", │ imageUrl: │ "]8;;https://images.example.com/product.jpg",https://images.example.com/product.jpg",]8;; │ price: 42, │ }, │ ], │ }), │ ), │ ], │ }; │ ``` │ ```tsx │ // .storybook/preview.tsx │ import { definePreview } from 'storybook/preview'; │ import { initialize, mswLoader } from 'msw-storybook-addon'; │ import { mswHandlers } from './msw-handlers'; │ initialize({ │ onUnhandledRequest: 'bypass', │ }); │ export default definePreview({ │ loaders: [mswLoader], │ parameters: { │ msw: { │ handlers: mswHandlers, │ }, │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ ```ts │ // .storybook/main.ts │ import type { StorybookConfig } from "@storybook/react-vite"; │ const config: StorybookConfig = { │ staticDirs: ["../public"], │ }; │ export default config; │ ``` │ Keep these mocks global. │ Do not put fetch mocks in individual stories. │ Only add handlers for requests that the shared preview setup or the │ stories actually use. │ Do not add catch-all handlers that can hide unrelated failures. │ If the defaults are not enough, improve the shared default setup │ instead. │ Seed browser state when needed, but do not mock `window`, `document`, │ `navigator`, observers, or similar runtime APIs. │ The only exception is `mockdate` when date-based rendering exists. │ ### Step 5: Write stories │ Try to find around 10 good candidate components for story files. │ Write colocated stories for top-level components, from low-level │ reusable components up to page components. │ Write up to 10 story files, or fewer only if the codebase clearly has │ fewer meaningful targets. │ The stories should use JSX copied from real usage patterns in: │ - pages │ - app shells │ - routes │ - tests │ - existing feature code │ As a rule of thumb, each story file should have around 3 story exports │ when the component or page has enough meaningful states. │ It can have more when the real usage supports it, up to 10 story │ exports in one file. │ Always show all imports explicitly in story and preview files. │ Do not rely on omitted or implied imports in examples or generated │ code. │ #### Story tags │ Every story meta must include the `ai-generated` tag to identify │ AI-created stories: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { SomeComponent } from './SomeComponent'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: SomeComponent, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const Default = meta.story({ │ render: () => , │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button')).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ If a story could not be fully fixed after the self-healing loop (the │ test still fails │ or the rendering is incomplete), add the `needs-work` tag alongside │ `ai-generated`: │ ```ts │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: SomeComponent, │ tags: ['ai-generated', 'needs-work'], │ }); │ ``` │ #### Args vs render │ For simple components where props drive the state, prefer `args` │ stories — no `render` function needed: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { Button } from './Button'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: Button, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const Primary = meta.story({ │ args: { │ variant: 'primary', │ children: 'Save', │ }, │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i │ })).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }); │ export const Disabled = meta.story({ │ args: { │ variant: 'primary', │ disabled: true, │ children: 'Save', │ }, │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button')).toBeDisabled(); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ Use `render` when the story needs composition — wrapping the component │ in layout, combining multiple components, or passing children as JSX: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { Button } from './Button'; │ import { Card } from './Card'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: Button, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const InsideCard = meta.story({ │ render: () => ( │ │ │ │ ), │ play: async ({ canvas, userEvent }) => { │ await expect(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i │ })).toBeVisible(); │ await userEvent.click(canvas.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i })); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ Keep app mocking and runtime setup in preview, not in the stories. │ Do not build large story-specific harnesses. │ Do not write story files for subcomponents, hooks, contexts, or │ helpers. │ Do not create new application components. │ Do not add a custom `title`. │ Do not stop after only a few easy targets if the codebase has more │ meaningful components or pages available. │ ### Step 6: Write a play function for every story │ Every named story export must have a `play` function. │ The `play` function is not optional, even for simple stories. │ The purpose of the `play` function is to prove that the story actually │ works in the copied Storybook environment: │ - the story renders something real and non-empty │ - the decorators provide the needed context │ - the CSS is applied well enough for the intended state to be visible │ - the MSW mocks or seeded browser state are actually being used │ - important interactions, async loading states, and portals behave │ correctly │ Use `play` functions to verify behavior, not just to click around. │ A story without assertions is incomplete. │ Use tools from `storybook/test` such as: │ - `expect` │ - `waitFor` │ Prefer `canvas` and `userEvent` from the `play` context. │ Do not destructure `canvasElement` just to create `const canvas = │ within(canvasElement)`. │ Do not import `userEvent` from `storybook/test`; use `userEvent` from │ the `play` context instead. │ Only use `canvasElement.ownerDocument` when you need to query outside │ the canvas, such as for portals. │ Example: │ ```tsx │ import type { StoryObj } from "@storybook/react-vite"; │ export const FilledForm: Story = { │ play: async ({ canvas, userEvent }) => { │ const emailInput = canvas.getByLabelText("email", { │ selector: "input", │ }); │ await userEvent.type(emailInput, "example-email@email.com", { │ delay: 100, │ }); │ const passwordInput = canvas.getByLabelText("password", { │ selector: "input", │ }); │ await userEvent.type(passwordInput, "ExamplePassword", { │ delay: 100, │ }); │ const submitButton = canvas.getByRole("button"); │ await userEvent.click(submitButton); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ The assertions should match the real pattern you copied: │ - for provider-backed stories, assert the provider-dependent UI │ appears correctly │ - for mocked-data stories, wait for the mocked data to appear and │ assert on it │ - for CSS-sensitive states, assert on visibility, text layout, │ class-driven states, or meaningful computed styles │ - for routing or navigation stories, assert the routed state or │ navigation outcome │ - for portal stories, query from `canvasElement.ownerDocument` when │ the UI renders outside the canvas │ Examples of useful checks: │ - a themed button has the expected label and is visibly enabled or │ disabled │ - a modal opened through a decorator or provider is visible in the │ portal root │ - mocked API data appears in the page instead of a loading spinner │ forever │ - a selected tab actually shows the selected panel │ - a toast, alert, or badge has the expected accessible text and visual │ state │ - a CSS class or computed style confirms the real state that matters │ ### Step 7: Prove CSS is loaded in exactly one story named `CssCheck` │ In exactly one story, named `CssCheck`, assert a component-specific │ computed style. `toBeVisible` passes on an unstyled component; a │ concrete style value proves the shared preview loaded the app's CSS. │ Pick a visually distinctive component, read a styling value from its │ source, and assert it with `getComputedStyle`: │ ```tsx │ export const CssCheck: Story = { │ args: { children: "Submit" }, │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ const button = canvas.getByRole("button", { name: /submit/i }); │ // PrimaryButton uses bg-blue-600 — fails if Tailwind / global CSS did │ not load. │ await expect(getComputedStyle(button).backgroundColor).toBe("rgb(37, │ 99, 235)"); │ }, │ }; │ ``` │ ### Step 8: Cover the patterns you found │ Write stories for the real patterns in the codebase, for example: │ - a low-level reusable component in real JSX usage │ - a provider-backed component │ - a browser-state-backed component │ - a fetched-data component │ - a real page component │ Use `App.tsx` to inspect the real provider tree and usage patterns, │ but do not make a story for `App` when the codebase has actual page │ components. │ Example page story: │ ```tsx │ import preview from '#.storybook/preview'; │ import { expect } from 'storybook/test'; │ import { ProductPage } from './ProductPage'; │ const meta = preview.meta({ │ component: ProductPage, │ tags: ['ai-generated'], │ }); │ export const Default = meta.story({ │ render: () => , │ play: async ({ canvas }) => { │ await expect( │ canvas.getByRole('heading', { name: /products/i }), │ ).toBeVisible(); │ }, │ }); │ ``` │ ### Step 9: Verify both rendering and types │ As you work, verify the stories with Vitest: │ ```bash │ npx vitest --project storybook │ ``` │ Also verify types so you catch missing required props, broken imports, │ and preview typing issues. Run the same TypeScript command the project │ itself uses. │ ```bash │ │ ``` │ After verification passes, review every changed file and remove │ anything that is not needed for the final solution, especially debug │ fixes, overly broad mocks, unnecessary dependencies, and eval │ artifacts. │ Keep iterating until: │ - every story you wrote passes │ - every story you wrote has a meaningful passing `play` function │ - the changed stories and preview setup pass the project's real │ TypeScript check │ - the rendered output looks sensible │ - the default global mocked environment is strong enough that stories │ do not need manual fetch overrides │ - stories no longer fail because the shared preview setup and story │ JSX are fixed │ - all passing stories have `tags: ['ai-generated']` in their meta │ - any stories that still need work have `tags: ['ai-generated', │ 'needs-work']` in their meta

{
  "name": "@evg-ui/lib",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "check-types": "tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit",
    "eslint:fix": "pnpm eslint:strict --fix",
    "eslint:staged": "STRICT=1 eslint",
    "eslint:strict": "STRICT=1 eslint .",
    "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6008",
    "storybook:build": "storybook build",
    "build-storybook": "storybook build",
    "test": "vitest",
    "codegen": "graphql-codegen-esm --config graphql.config.ts",
    "chromatic": "chromatic --exit-zero-on-changes --only-changed --build-script-name storybook:build",
    "vitest:storybook": "vitest run --project=storybook"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@apollo/client": "^4.1.3",
    "@emotion/react": "11.14.0",
    "@emotion/styled": "11.14.1",

     6→    '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
     7→    './eval-support/*.mdx',
     8→  ],
     9→  addons: [
    10→    '@chromatic-com/storybook',
    11→    '@storybook/addon-vitest',
    12→    '@storybook/addon-a11y',
    13→    '@storybook/addon-docs',
    14→    '@storybook/addon-onboarding',
    15→  ],
    16→  framework: '@storybook/react-vite',
    17→};
    18→
    19→export default config;

1→# Analytics Module
     2→
     3→Type-safe event tracking for user interactions. Built on the [Observability Package](../utils/observability/README.md).
     4→
     5→> **Note:** The observability package handles infrastructure telemetry (page loads, API calls). Use analytics for **business-specific user actions** (button clicks, feature usage).
     6→
     7→> **Analytics Events Catalog:** All tracked analytics events are documented in the [Evergreen Analytics Spreadsheet](http://go/evergreen-analytics).
     8→
     9→## Quick Start
    10→
    11→### 1. Create an analytics hook
    12→
    13→```typescript
    14→// apps/spruce/src/analytics/task/useTaskAnalytics.ts
    15→import { useAnalyticsRoot } from "@evg-ui/lib/analytics/hooks";
    16→
    17→type Action =
    18→  | { name: "Clicked restart button" }
    19→  | { name: "Changed task priority"; "task.priority": number }
    20→  | { name: "Filtered tests table"; "filter.by": string };
    21→
    22→export const useTaskAnalytics = () => {
    23→  const { taskId } = useParams();
    24→
    25→  return useAnalyticsRoot<Action, string>("Task", {
    26→    "task.id": taskId,
    27→  });
    28→};
    29→```
    30→
    31→### 2. Use in your component
    32→
    33→```typescript
    34→import { useTaskAnalytics } from "analytics/task/useTaskAnalytics";
    35→
    36→const TaskPage = () => {
    37→  const { sendEvent } = useTaskAnalytics();
    38→
    39→  const handleRestart = () => {
    40→    sendEvent({ name: "Clicked restart button" });
    41→    // ... restart logic
    42→  };
    43→
    44→  return <Button onClick={handleRestart}>Restart</Button>;
    45→};
    46→```
    47→
    48→## Action Type Requirements
    49→
    50→Event names must start with one of these prefixes:
    51→
    52→- `Clicked` - Button/link clicks
    53→- `Changed` - Value/state changes
    54→- `Filtered` - Filtering operations
    55→- `Sorted` - Sorting operations
    56→- `Toggled` - Toggle actions
    57→- `Viewed` - Page/modal views
    58→- `Created` - Resource creation
    59→- `Deleted` - Resource deletion
    60→- `Saved` - Save operations
    61→- `Used` - Feature usage
    62→
    63→## What Gets Tracked
    64→
    65→Each analytics event automatically includes:
    66→
    67→**From your action:**
    68→- Event name and custom properties
    69→- Analytics identifier (e.g., "Task")
    70→- Default properties from `useAnalyticsRoot()`
    71→
    72→**From observability (automatic):**
    73→- User ID, environment, app version
    74→- Current route and route parameters
    75→- Trace ID and span ID
    76→
    77→## API Reference
    78→
    79→### `useAnalyticsRoot<Action, Identifier>(identifier, attributes?)`
    80→
    81→Creates an analytics hook for a feature/page.
    82→
    83→**Parameters:**
    84→- `identifier` - Feature name (e.g., "Task", "Navbar")
    85→- `attributes` - Optional default properties for all events
    86→
    87→**Returns:**
    88→- `{ sendEvent }` - Function to send analytics events
    89→
    90→### `sendEvent(action)`
    91→
    92→Sends an analytics event.
    93→
    94→**Example:**
    95→```typescript
    96→sendEvent({
    97→  name: "Filtered tests table",
    98→  "filter.by": "failed"
    99→});
   100→```
   101→
   102→## Best Practices
   103→
   104→### 1. One hook per feature/page
   105→```
   106→analytics/
   107→  ├── task/useTaskAnalytics.ts
   108→  ├── navbar/useNavbarAnalytics.ts
   109→  └── preferences/usePreferencesAnalytics.ts
   110→```
   111→
   112→### 2. Type your actions
   113→```typescript
   114→type Action =
   115→  | { name: "Clicked save button" }
   116→  | { name: "Changed priority"; priority: number };
   117→```
   118→
   119→### 3. Use descriptive names
   120→- ✅ "Clicked restart task button"
   121→- ❌ "Button clicked"
   122→
   123→### 4. Add relevant context
   124→```typescript
   125→sendEvent({
   126→  name: "Filtered tests table",
   127→  "filter.by": "failed",
   128→  "test.count": 42
   129→});
   130→```
   131→
   132→### 5. Never sample analytics events
   133→Unlike infrastructure traces, analytics events are:
   134→- Low volume (you choose what to track)
   135→- High value (direct user actions)
   136→- Essential for product insights
   137→
   138→If you have too many analytics events, track fewer types - don't sample.
   139→
   140→## Troubleshooting
   141→
   142→### Events not appearing
   143→1. Verify [observability package](../utils/observability/README.md) is initialized
   144→2. Check `window.AttributeStore` exists
   145→3. Look for errors in browser console
   146→4. Search Honeycomb for `analytics.identifier` attribute
   147→
   148→### TypeScript errors
   149→- Ensure action names start with required prefixes
   150→- Verify all action properties are in your `Action` type
   151→
   152→## Examples
   153→
   154→### Simple hook
   155→```typescript
   156→type Action =
   157→  | { name: "Clicked logo" }
   158→  | { name: "Clicked settings" };
   159→
   160→export const useNavbarAnalytics = () =>
   161→  useAnalyticsRoot<Action, string>("Navbar");
   162→```
   163→
   164→### Hook with context
   165→```typescript
   166→type Action =
   167→  | { name: "Clicked restart" }
   168→  | { name: "Changed priority"; priority: number };
   169→
   170→export const useTaskAnalytics = () => {
   171→  const { taskId } = useParams();
   172→  const { data } = useQuery(TASK_QUERY);
   173→
   174→  return useAnalyticsRoot<Action, string>("Task", {
   175→    "task.id": taskId,
   176→    "task.status": data?.task?.status,
   177→  });
   178→};
   179→```
   180→
   181→### Usage in component
   182→```typescript
   183→const TaskActions = () => {
   184→  const { sendEvent } = useTaskAnalytics();
   185→
   186→  return (
   187→    <>
   188→      <Button onClick={() => sendEvent({ name: "Clicked restart" })}>
   189→        Restart
   190→      </Button>
   191→      <Select onChange={(priority) =>
   192→        sendEvent({ name: "Changed priority", priority })
   193→      } />
   194→    </>
   195→  );
   196→};
   197→```
   198→
   199→## Related Documentation
   200→
   201→- [Observability Package](../utils/observability/README.md) - Infrastructure telemetry
   202→- [Honeycomb](https://docs.honeycomb.io/) - Query and analyze your events

1→import { useCallback, useMemo } from "react";
     2→import { Analytics, ActionType, AnalyticsProperties } from "./types";
     3→import { sendEventTrace } from "./utils";
     4→
     5→/**
     6→ *
     7→ * This is simply a clever named type to help with the typing of the `useAnalyticsRoot` hook.
     8→ * `AnalyticsIdentifier` is a string that represents the object to send with an event to our analytics provider.
     9→ * @example - "Page Name"
    10→ * @example - "Component Name"
    11→ */
    12→type AnalyticsIdentifier = string;
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * `useAnalyticsRoot` is a hook that returns a function to send an event to our analytics provider
    16→ * @param analyticsIdentifier - The identifier to send with the event this is typically the page name or component name we are tracking
    17→ * @param attributes - The additional properties that will be passed into our analytics event.
    18→ * @returns - The sendEvent function to send an event to our analytics provider
    19→ */
    20→export const useAnalyticsRoot = <
    21→  Action extends ActionType,
    22→  Identifier extends AnalyticsIdentifier,
    23→>(
    24→  analyticsIdentifier: Identifier,
    25→  attributes: AnalyticsProperties = {},
    26→): Analytics<Action> => {
    27→  const sendEvent: Analytics<Action>["sendEvent"] = useCallback(
    28→    (action) => {
    29→      sendEventTrace(action, {
    30→        "analytics.identifier": analyticsIdentifier,
    31→        ...attributes,
    32→      });
    33→    },
    34→    [analyticsIdentifier, attributes],
    35→  );
    36→
    37→  return useMemo(() => ({ sendEvent }), [sendEvent]);
    38→};
    39→

1→import type { AttributeValue } from "@opentelemetry/api";
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * `ActionTypePrefixes` is a union type of all the prefixes that can be used to create an `ActionType`.
     5→ */
     6→type ActionTypePrefixes =
     7→  | "Changed"
     8→  | "Clicked"
     9→  | "Created"
    10→  | "Deleted"
    11→  | "Redirected"
    12→  | "Filtered"
    13→  | "Saved"
    14→  | "Sorted"
    15→  | "Toggled"
    16→  | "Viewed"
    17→  | "Used"
    18→  | "System Event"
    19→  | "Interacted with";
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * `ActionType` is a type that represents an action that can be sent to our analytics provider.
    23→ * @param name - The name of the action to send to our analytics provider
    24→ * @example - { name: "Clicked Button" }
    25→ * @example - { name: "Changed Input" }
    26→ */
    27→export interface ActionType {
    28→  name: `${ActionTypePrefixes}${string}`;
    29→}
    30→
    31→/**
    32→ * `sendEvent` is the function call to send an analytics event. It requires an Action
    33→ */
    34→type sendEvent<Action extends ActionType> = (action: Action) => void;
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * `AnalyticsProperties` is an object that represents the properties and additional metadata to send with an event to our analytics provider.
    38→ *
    39→ */
    40→export interface AnalyticsProperties {
    41→  [key: string]: AttributeValue;
    42→}
    43→
    44→/**
    45→ * `Analytics` is an object that represents the analytics provider and the function to send an event to the provider.
    46→ */
    47→export interface Analytics<Action extends ActionType> {
    48→  sendEvent: sendEvent<Action>;
    49→}
    50→
    51→/**
    52→ * `ExtractAnalyticsSendEvent` is a utility type that can be used to extract the sendEvent function from an analytics hook.
    53→ */
    54→export type ExtractAnalyticsSendEvent<A> = A extends () => Analytics<
    55→  infer Action
    56→>
    57→  ? Analytics<Action>["sendEvent"]
    58→  : never;
    59→

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary document.

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: @evg-ui/lib

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Root Directory:** `/Users/yannbraga/open-source/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-28T22-33-19Z-8d39fffa/project/packages/lib`

**Key Directories:**
- `/src` - Main source code
  - `/components` - Reusable UI components (17 directories)
  - `/context` - React Context providers (AuthProvider, Toast)
  - `/hooks` - Custom React hooks
  - `/pages` - Page-level components (LoginPage)
  - `/utils` - Utility functions and helpers
  - `/constants` - Application constants
  - `/gql` - GraphQL queries, mutations, and generated types
  - `/analytics` - Analytics tracking system
  - `/types` - TypeScript type definitions
  - `/stories` - Storybook examples
  - `/test_utils` - Testing utilities
- `/.storybook` - Storybook configuration
- `/config` - Build and test configuration
- `/scripts` - Build scripts

### 2. ENTRY POINT & PROVIDERS

This is a **UI library package**, not a standalone application. There is no traditional `main.tsx` or `App.tsx` file. Instead:

- **No central app entry point** - This is a component library exported from individual components
- **Storybook Configuration:** `.storybook/main.ts`
  - Uses `@storybook/react-vite` framework
  - Loads stories from `../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)`
  - Addons: chromatic, vitest, a11y, docs, onboarding
  
- **Storybook Preview:** `.storybook/preview.tsx`
  - Minimal configuration with controls matchers for color and date
  - A11y test set to 'todo'

### 3. PROVIDER TREE & CONTEXT

**AuthProvider** (`/src/context/AuthProvider/index.tsx`)
- Manages authentication state with support for both local and remote auth strategies
- **State:**
  - `isAuthenticated: boolean` - Whether user is currently authenticated
  - `hasCheckedAuth: boolean` - Whether auth status has been verified
- **Methods:**
  - `localLogin(credentials)` - Login with username/password (dev only)
  - `logoutAndRedirect()` - Logout and redirect to remote auth URL or local auth route
  - `dispatchAuthenticated()` - Mark user as authenticated
- **Data Flow:**
  - On mount: Fetches user data via GraphQL to verify authentication
  - Uses `fetchWithRetry` for robust auth checks
  - Stores staging environment in cookies (`evg-staging-environment`)
  - Integrates with Sentry for error tracking

**ToastProvider** (`/src/context/toast/index.tsx`)
- Wraps LeafyGreen's `ToastProvider`
- **Methods:**
  - `success(message, closable, options)` - Success toast
  - `error(message, closable, options)` - Error/warning toast
  - `warning(message, closable, options)` - Important/warning variant
  - `info(message, closable, options)` - Note/info variant
  - `progress(message, progress, closable, options)` - Progress variant
- **Configuration:**
  - Default timeout: 3000ms (from `TOAST_TIMEOUT`)
  - Uses Emotion for styling
  - Custom `WordBreak` component for message display

### 4. CONTEXT PROVIDERS SUMMARY

| Provider | Location | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| AuthProvider | `/src/context/AuthProvider/index.tsx` | User authentication management |
| ToastProvider | `/src/context/toast/index.tsx` | Toast notifications (wraps LeafyGreen) |

### 5. HOOKS THAT FETCH DATA

**GraphQL Hooks:**
- `useAdminBetaFeatures()` - Queries `ADMIN_BETA_FEATURES`
- `useUserBetaFeatures()` - Queries `USER_BETA_FEATURES`
- `useMergedBetaFeatures()` - Merges admin and user beta features

**Custom Data Hooks:**
- `useBetaFeatures/index.ts` - Three hooks for beta feature management using Apollo
- `useErrorToast()` - Displays error toasts from GraphQL query errors with deduplication
- `useQueryParam()` - URL query parameter state management
- `useQueryParams()` - Multi-parameter URL state management

**Other Hooks (Non-Data):**
- `useDebouncedCallback()` - Debounced callbacks
- `useKeyboardShortcut()` - Keyboard shortcut handling
- `useOnClickOutside()` - Detect clicks outside element
- `usePageTitle()` - Update page title
- `usePrevious()` - Track previous value
- `usePagination()` - Pagination state

**GraphQL Setup:**
- Uses Apollo Client v4.1.3
- Queries: `admin-beta-features.graphql`, `user-beta-features.graphql`
- Mutations: `update-user-beta-features.graphql`
- Code generation: GraphQL codegen configured in `graphql.config.ts`
- Generated types at: `/src/gql/generated/types.ts`

### 6. BROWSER STATE READS

**localStorage:**
- Wrapper functions in `/src/utils/localStorage/index.ts`:
  - `getLocalStorageString(key)` - Retrieve string value
  - `setLocalStorageString(key, value)` - Store string value
  - `getLocalStorageBoolean(key, defaultValue?)` - Retrieve boolean
  - `setLocalStorageBoolean(key, value)` - Store boolean
  - All wrapped with try-catch to handle unavailable/full storage

**Cookies:**
- Uses `js-cookie` v3.0.5
- `evg-staging-environment` cookie - Stores staging environment key (365 day expiration)
- Set in `AuthProvider.logoutAndRedirect()` when staging is active
- Retrieved via `getUserStagingKey()` from environment variables utility

**No sessionStorage usage detected in codebase**

### 7. PORTAL USAGE

**No `createPortal` usage found in component files.**

Portal-like behavior is provided by:
- **LeafyGreen Toast:** `LGToastProvider` has `portalClassName` prop - configurable DOM portal location
- **LeafyGreen Tooltip/Popover:** Used internally by components like `Popconfirm`

### 8. PAGE-LEVEL COMPONENTS

**Current Pages:**
- `LoginPage` at `/src/pages/LoginPage/index.tsx`
  - Props: `ignoreAuthCheck?: boolean` (for test environments)
  - Manages username/password local login
  - Redirects authenticated users or shows login form
  - Uses `useAuthProviderContext()` for auth state and methods
  - Styled with Emotion
  - Uses LeafyGreen TextInput and Button components

**No other page components currently exist** - This is primarily a component library.

### 9. REUSABLE UI COMPONENTS

**Total: 57 component files**

**Component Directories:**

1. **Accordion** - Collapsible section with animation
   - Props: `open`, `defaultOpen`, `title`, `subtitle`, `caretIcon`, `caretAlign`, etc.
   - Supports controlled/uncontrolled modes
   - Customizable caret icon (Caret or Chevron)

2. **Badge** - Status indicators
   - `TaskStatusBadge` - Task status display
   - `TestStatusBadge` - Test status display

3. **ErrorBoundary** - Error handling wrapper
   - Delegates to `SentryErrorBoundary` if Sentry initialized
   - Falls back to `DefaultErrorBoundary`
   - Requires `homeURL` prop

4. **ExpiringAnnouncementTooltip** - Tooltip that expires after viewing

5. **FullPageLoad** - Full-screen loading indicator
   - Simple centered "LOADING..." text

6. **Icon** - Icon rendering
   - `AnimatedIcon` - Icon with animations
   - `/icons` - Icon definitions

7. **IconWithTooltip** - Icon with hover tooltip

8. **PageSizeSelector** - Pagination size selector

9. **Pagination** - Page navigation (wraps LeafyGreen)

10. **Popconfirm** - Confirmation popover
    - Props: `children`, `onConfirm`, `confirmText`, `confirmDisabled`, `open`, `setOpen`
    - Controlled/uncontrolled modes
    - Uses LeafyGreen Tooltip internally
    - Click-outside detection

11. **ProtectedRoute** - Route guard
    - Checks auth status via `useAuthProviderContext()`
    - Redirects to login if not authenticated
    - Preserves referrer location state

12. **Table** - Data table component
    - **BaseTable** - Core table implementation
    - **TableWrapper** - Simple wrapper
    - **TableControl** - Table controls/toolbar
    - **TableLoader** - Loading skeleton
    - **TablePlaceholder** - Empty state
    - **TablePopover** - Filtering/searching popovers
    - Features:
      - Pagination support
      - Column filtering and searching
      - Row selection, sorting, expansion
      - Virtual scrolling support
      - Uses TanStack Table v8 and LeafyGreen table
      - Tree select filter options
      - Custom column metadata

13. **TextInputWithGlyph** - Input with icon prefix

14. **TreeSelect** - Hierarchical select component
    - **FilterInputControls** - Search and controls for tree
    - Props include `filterOptions` to filter tree by table values
    - Test file shows comprehensive testing

15. **styles** - Global and reusable styles
    - `Typography`, `Link`, `fonts`, `globalStyles`

### 10. PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCIES & SCRIPTS

**Key Dependencies:**

**UI Framework & Components:**
- `react` 18.3.1, `react-dom` 18.3.1
- `@leafygreen-ui/*` - MongoDB design system (buttons, tables, tooltips, etc.)
- `@emotion/react` 11.14.0, `@emotion/styled` 11.14.1 - CSS-in-JS

**State & Data:**
- `@apollo/client` 4.1.3 - GraphQL client
- `graphql` 16.12.0 - GraphQL library
- `@tanstack/table-core` 8.20.5 - Headless table library

**Routing:**
- `react-router-dom` 6.18.0

**Utilities:**
- `js-cookie` 3.0.5 - Cookie management
- `query-string` 9.1.1 - Query string parsing
- `date-fns` 4.1.0 - Date manipulation

**Observability:**
- `@sentry/react` 10.31.0 - Error tracking
- `@opentelemetry/*` - Observability/telemetry
- `@honeycombio/opentelemetry-web` 1.1.0 - Honeycomb telemetry

**Scripts:**
```json
{
  "check-types": "tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit",
  "eslint:fix": "pnpm eslint:strict --fix",
  "eslint:staged": "STRICT=1 eslint",
  "eslint:strict": "STRICT=1 eslint .",
  "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6008",
  "storybook:build": "storybook build",
  "build-storybook": "storybook build",
  "test": "vitest",
  "codegen": "graphql-codegen-esm --config graphql.config.ts",
  "chromatic": "chromatic --exit-zero-on-changes --only-changed --build-script-name storybook:build",
  "vitest:storybook": "vitest run --project=storybook"
}
```

### 11. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION

**File:** `.storybook/main.ts`

```typescript
{
  stories: [
    '../src/**/*.mdx',
    '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
    './eval-support/*.mdx',
  ],
  addons: [
    '@chromatic-com/storybook',
    '@storybook/addon-vitest',
    '@storybook/addon-a11y',
    '@storybook/addon-docs',
    '@storybook/addon-onboarding',
  ],
  framework: '@storybook/react-vite'
}
```

**Features:**
- Vitest integration for component testing
- Accessibility testing with a11y addon
- Chromatic visual regression testing
- Markdown documentation support

### 12. TSCONFIG.JSON

**File:** `/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "include": [
    "src",
    "test_utils/index.tsx",
    "test_utils/custom-queries.ts",
    "./config/vitest/setupTests.ts",
    ".storybook/preview.tsx",
    "src/**/*.d.ts",
    "vitest.d.ts"
  ],
  "compilerOptions": {
    "paths": {
      "*": ["./src/*"]  // Alias all imports to src/*
    }
  }
}
```

### 13. TEST SETUP

**Vitest Configuration:** `vitest.config.ts`

```
- Two projects:
  1. JSDOM environment (standard unit tests)
  2. Storybook project (browser-based tests with Playwright)
```

**Test Projects:**
- **Default:** jsdom environment
  - Setup file: `config/vitest/setupTests.ts`
  - Global setup: `config/vitest/global-setup.ts`
- **Storybook:** Browser-based with Playwright
  - Headless Chromium
  - Optional mobile device emulation (iPhone 17 at 2x DPI)
  - Screenshots support via `STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS` env var

**Setup File:** `config/vitest/setupTests.ts`

```typescript
- Imports @testing-library/jest-dom
- Imports vitest-canvas-mock for canvas support
- Polyfills jest global for @testing-library/react compatibility
- Mocks HTMLElement.showPopover/hidePopover for jsdom 28
```

**Test Utilities:** `/src/test_utils/index.tsx`

- `render()` / `customRender()` - Enhanced render with custom queries
- `renderWithRouterMatch()` - Render with React Router memory router
- `renderComponentWithHook()` - Test hook utility
- `customScreen` - screen object with custom queries bound
- Custom queries and within
- `MockedProvider` from @apollo/client for GraphQL mocking
- `createWrapper()` - Generic wrapper factory
- Utilities: `act`, `fireEvent`, `userEvent`, `waitFor`, `within`

**Custom Test Types:** `/src/test_utils/types.ts`

```typescript
- ApolloMock<Data, Variables> - GraphQL mock definition
- CustomStoryObj<T> - Story with custom parameters
- CustomMeta<T> - Meta with custom parameters
- Parameters include: reactRouter (route, path, initialEntries), apolloClient (mocks)
```

### 14. ADDITIONAL UTILITIES & SYSTEMS

**Analytics System** (`/src/analytics/`)
- `useAnalyticsRoot<Action, Identifier>(identifier, attributes?)` - Type-safe analytics hook
- Tracks user actions: Clicked, Changed, Created, Deleted, Filtered, Sorted, Toggled, Viewed, Saved, Used, etc.
- Integrates with OpenTelemetry for tracing
- Sends events to Honeycomb with automatic user/environment/route context

**Error Reporting** (`/src/utils/errorReporting/`)
- Sentry integration for error tracking
- Breadcrumb utilities for debugging

**Request Utilities** (`/src/utils/request/`)
- `fetchWithRetry()` - Fetch with retry logic
- `getUserStagingHeader()` - Staging environment headers

**Environment Variables** (`/src/utils/environmentVariables/`)
- `getUserStagingKey()` - Get user's staging environment
- `isStaging()` - Check if staging mode enabled

**Other Utilities:**
- `query-string` - URL parameter parsing/stringification
- `pagination` - Pagination helpers
- `array` - Array utility functions
- `string` - String utility functions
- `object` - Object utility functions
- `streams` - Stream handling
- `observability` - OpenTelemetry integration with custom processors

### 15. GRAPHQL CONFIGURATION

**File:** `graphql.config.ts`

```
- Documents: './src/**/*.graphql'
- Output: './src/gql/generated/types.ts'
- Schema: 'sdlschema/**/*.graphql'
- Plugins: typescript, typescript-operations
- Scalars: Duration (number), StringMap (object), Time (Date)
- Auto-format with Prettier
```

**GraphQL Files:**
- Queries: `admin-beta-features.graphql`, `user-beta-features.graphql`
- Mutations: `update-user-beta-features.graphql`

---

## Summary

This is a **React component library package (@evg-ui/lib)** built with TypeScript, Emotion styling, and LeafyGreen UI components. It provides:

1. **State Management:** React Context (Auth, Toast)
2. **Data Fetching:** Apollo Client with GraphQL
3. **UI Components:** 15+ reusable components (Table, TreeSelect, Accordion, etc.)
4. **Testing:** Vitest with jsdom and browser-based Playwright testing
5. **Documentation:** Storybook with Chromatic integration
6. **Analytics:** Type-safe event tracking with OpenTelemetry
7. **Error Tracking:** Sentry integration
8. **Observability:** Honeycomb telemetry system

The library supports authentication (local and remote), routing with protected routes, toast notifications, and comprehensive data table functionality with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed summary document.

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis: @evg-ui/lib

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Root Directory:** `/Users/yannbraga/open-source/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-28T22-33-19Z-8d39fffa/project/packages/lib`

**Key Directories:**
- `/src` - Main source code
  - `/components` - Reusable UI components (17 directories)
  - `/context` - React Context providers (AuthProvider, Toast)
  - `/hooks` - Custom React hooks
  - `/pages` - Page-level components (LoginPage)
  - `/utils` - Utility functions and helpers
  - `/constants` - Application constants
  - `/gql` - GraphQL queries, mutations, and generated types
  - `/analytics` - Analytics tracking system
  - `/types` - TypeScript type definitions
  - `/stories` - Storybook examples
  - `/test_utils` - Testing utilities
- `/.storybook` - Storybook configuration
- `/config` - Build and test configuration
- `/scripts` - Build scripts

### 2. ENTRY POINT & PROVIDERS

This is a **UI library package**, not a standalone application. There is no traditional `main.tsx` or `App.tsx` file. Instead:

- **No central app entry point** - This is a component library exported from individual components
- **Storybook Configuration:** `.storybook/main.ts`
  - Uses `@storybook/react-vite` framework
  - Loads stories from `../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)`
  - Addons: chromatic, vitest, a11y, docs, onboarding
  
- **Storybook Preview:** `.storybook/preview.tsx`
  - Minimal configuration with controls matchers for color and date
  - A11y test set to 'todo'

### 3. PROVIDER TREE & CONTEXT

**AuthProvider** (`/src/context/AuthProvider/index.tsx`)
- Manages authentication state with support for both local and remote auth strategies
- **State:**
  - `isAuthenticated: boolean` - Whether user is currently authenticated
  - `hasCheckedAuth: boolean` - Whether auth status has been verified
- **Methods:**
  - `localLogin(credentials)` - Login with username/password (dev only)
  - `logoutAndRedirect()` - Logout and redirect to remote auth URL or local auth route
  - `dispatchAuthenticated()` - Mark user as authenticated
- **Data Flow:**
  - On mount: Fetches user data via GraphQL to verify authentication
  - Uses `fetchWithRetry` for robust auth checks
  - Stores staging environment in cookies (`evg-staging-environment`)
  - Integrates with Sentry for error tracking

**ToastProvider** (`/src/context/toast/index.tsx`)
- Wraps LeafyGreen's `ToastProvider`
- **Methods:**
  - `success(message, closable, options)` - Success toast
  - `error(message, closable, options)` - Error/warning toast
  - `warning(message, closable, options)` - Important/warning variant
  - `info(message, closable, options)` - Note/info variant
  - `progress(message, progress, closable, options)` - Progress variant
- **Configuration:**
  - Default timeout: 3000ms (from `TOAST_TIMEOUT`)
  - Uses Emotion for styling
  - Custom `WordBreak` component for message display

### 4. CONTEXT PROVIDERS SUMMARY

| Provider | Location | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| AuthProvider | `/src/context/AuthProvider/index.tsx` | User authentication management |
| ToastProvider | `/src/context/toast/index.tsx` | Toast notifications (wraps LeafyGreen) |

### 5. HOOKS THAT FETCH DATA

**GraphQL Hooks:**
- `useAdminBetaFeatures()` - Queries `ADMIN_BETA_FEATURES`
- `useUserBetaFeatures()` - Queries `USER_BETA_FEATURES`
- `useMergedBetaFeatures()` - Merges admin and user beta features

**Custom Data Hooks:**
- `useBetaFeatures/index.ts` - Three hooks for beta feature management using Apollo
- `useErrorToast()` - Displays error toasts from GraphQL query errors with deduplication
- `useQueryParam()` - URL query parameter state management
- `useQueryParams()` - Multi-parameter URL state management

**Other Hooks (Non-Data):**
- `useDebouncedCallback()` - Debounced callbacks
- `useKeyboardShortcut()` - Keyboard shortcut handling
- `useOnClickOutside()` - Detect clicks outside element
- `usePageTitle()` - Update page title
- `usePrevious()` - Track previous value
- `usePagination()` - Pagination state

**GraphQL Setup:**
- Uses Apollo Client v4.1.3
- Queries: `admin-beta-features.graphql`, `user-beta-features.graphql`
- Mutations: `update-user-beta-features.graphql`
- Code generation: GraphQL codegen configured in `graphql.config.ts`
- Generated types at: `/src/gql/generated/types.ts`

### 6. BROWSER STATE READS

**localStorage:**
- Wrapper functions in `/src/utils/localStorage/index.ts`:
  - `getLocalStorageString(key)` - Retrieve string value
  - `setLocalStorageString(key, value)` - Store string value
  - `getLocalStorageBoolean(key, defaultValue?)` - Retrieve boolean
  - `setLocalStorageBoolean(key, value)` - Store boolean
  - All wrapped with try-catch to handle unavailable/full storage

**Cookies:**
- Uses `js-cookie` v3.0.5
- `evg-staging-environment` cookie - Stores staging environment key (365 day expiration)
- Set in `AuthProvider.logoutAndRedirect()` when staging is active
- Retrieved via `getUserStagingKey()` from environment variables utility

**No sessionStorage usage detected in codebase**

### 7. PORTAL USAGE

**No `createPortal` usage found in component files.**

Portal-like behavior is provided by:
- **LeafyGreen Toast:** `LGToastProvider` has `portalClassName` prop - configurable DOM portal location
- **LeafyGreen Tooltip/Popover:** Used internally by components like `Popconfirm`

### 8. PAGE-LEVEL COMPONENTS

**Current Pages:**
- `LoginPage` at `/src/pages/LoginPage/index.tsx`
  - Props: `ignoreAuthCheck?: boolean` (for test environments)
  - Manages username/password local login
  - Redirects authenticated users or shows login form
  - Uses `useAuthProviderContext()` for auth state and methods
  - Styled with Emotion
  - Uses LeafyGreen TextInput and Button components

**No other page components currently exist** - This is primarily a component library.

### 9. REUSABLE UI COMPONENTS

**Total: 57 component files**

**Component Directories:**

1. **Accordion** - Collapsible section with animation
   - Props: `open`, `defaultOpen`, `title`, `subtitle`, `caretIcon`, `caretAlign`, etc.
   - Supports controlled/uncontrolled modes
   - Customizable caret icon (Caret or Chevron)

2. **Badge** - Status indicators
   - `TaskStatusBadge` - Task status display
   - `TestStatusBadge` - Test status display

3. **ErrorBoundary** - Error handling wrapper
   - Delegates to `SentryErrorBoundary` if Sentry initialized
   - Falls back to `DefaultErrorBoundary`
   - Requires `homeURL` prop

4. **ExpiringAnnouncementTooltip** - Tooltip that expires after viewing

5. **FullPageLoad** - Full-screen loading indicator
   - Simple centered "LOADING..." text

6. **Icon** - Icon rendering
   - `AnimatedIcon` - Icon with animations
   - `/icons` - Icon definitions

7. **IconWithTooltip** - Icon with hover tooltip

8. **PageSizeSelector** - Pagination size selector

9. **Pagination** - Page navigation (wraps LeafyGreen)

10. **Popconfirm** - Confirmation popover
    - Props: `children`, `onConfirm`, `confirmText`, `confirmDisabled`, `open`, `setOpen`
    - Controlled/uncontrolled modes
    - Uses LeafyGreen Tooltip internally
    - Click-outside detection

11. **ProtectedRoute** - Route guard
    - Checks auth status via `useAuthProviderContext()`
    - Redirects to login if not authenticated
    - Preserves referrer location state

12. **Table** - Data table component
    - **BaseTable** - Core table implementation
    - **TableWrapper** - Simple wrapper
    - **TableControl** - Table controls/toolbar
    - **TableLoader** - Loading skeleton
    - **TablePlaceholder** - Empty state
    - **TablePopover** - Filtering/searching popovers
    - Features:
      - Pagination support
      - Column filtering and searching
      - Row selection, sorting, expansion
      - Virtual scrolling support
      - Uses TanStack Table v8 and LeafyGreen table
      - Tree select filter options
      - Custom column metadata

13. **TextInputWithGlyph** - Input with icon prefix

14. **TreeSelect** - Hierarchical select component
    - **FilterInputControls** - Search and controls for tree
    - Props include `filterOptions` to filter tree by table values
    - Test file shows comprehensive testing

15. **styles** - Global and reusable styles
    - `Typography`, `Link`, `fonts`, `globalStyles`

### 10. PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCIES & SCRIPTS

**Key Dependencies:**

**UI Framework & Components:**
- `react` 18.3.1, `react-dom` 18.3.1
- `@leafygreen-ui/*` - MongoDB design system (buttons, tables, tooltips, etc.)
- `@emotion/react` 11.14.0, `@emotion/styled` 11.14.1 - CSS-in-JS

**State & Data:**
- `@apollo/client` 4.1.3 - GraphQL client
- `graphql` 16.12.0 - GraphQL library
- `@tanstack/table-core` 8.20.5 - Headless table library

**Routing:**
- `react-router-dom` 6.18.0

**Utilities:**
- `js-cookie` 3.0.5 - Cookie management
- `query-string` 9.1.1 - Query string parsing
- `date-fns` 4.1.0 - Date manipulation

**Observability:**
- `@sentry/react` 10.31.0 - Error tracking
- `@opentelemetry/*` - Observability/telemetry
- `@honeycombio/opentelemetry-web` 1.1.0 - Honeycomb telemetry

**Scripts:**
```json
{
  "check-types": "tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit",
  "eslint:fix": "pnpm eslint:strict --fix",
  "eslint:staged": "STRICT=1 eslint",
  "eslint:strict": "STRICT=1 eslint .",
  "storybook": "storybook dev -p 6008",
  "storybook:build": "storybook build",
  "build-storybook": "storybook build",
  "test": "vitest",
  "codegen": "graphql-codegen-esm --config graphql.config.ts",
  "chromatic": "chromatic --exit-zero-on-changes --only-changed --build-script-name storybook:build",
  "vitest:storybook": "vitest run --project=storybook"
}
```

### 11. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION

**File:** `.storybook/main.ts`

```typescript
{
  stories: [
    '../src/**/*.mdx',
    '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)',
    './eval-support/*.mdx',
  ],
  addons: [
    '@chromatic-com/storybook',
    '@storybook/addon-vitest',
    '@storybook/addon-a11y',
    '@storybook/addon-docs',
    '@storybook/addon-onboarding',
  ],
  framework: '@storybook/react-vite'
}
```

**Features:**
- Vitest integration for component testing
- Accessibility testing with a11y addon
- Chromatic visual regression testing
- Markdown documentation support

### 12. TSCONFIG.JSON

**File:** `/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "include": [
    "src",
    "test_utils/index.tsx",
    "test_utils/custom-queries.ts",
    "./config/vitest/setupTests.ts",
    ".storybook/preview.tsx",
    "src/**/*.d.ts",
    "vitest.d.ts"
  ],
  "compilerOptions": {
    "paths": {
      "*": ["./src/*"]  // Alias all imports to src/*
    }
  }
}
```

### 13. TEST SETUP

**Vitest Configuration:** `vitest.config.ts`

```
- Two projects:
  1. JSDOM environment (standard unit tests)
  2. Storybook project (browser-based tests with Playwright)
```

**Test Projects:**
- **Default:** jsdom environment
  - Setup file: `config/vitest/setupTests.ts`
  - Global setup: `config/vitest/global-setup.ts`
- **Storybook:** Browser-based with Playwright
  - Headless Chromium
  - Optional mobile device emulation (iPhone 17 at 2x DPI)
  - Screenshots support via `STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS` env var

**Setup File:** `config/vitest/setupTests.ts`

```typescript
- Imports @testing-library/jest-dom
- Imports vitest-canvas-mock for canvas support
- Polyfills jest global for @testing-library/react compatibility
- Mocks HTMLElement.showPopover/hidePopover for jsdom 28
```

**Test Utilities:** `/src/test_utils/index.tsx`

- `render()` / `customRender()` - Enhanced render with custom queries
- `renderWithRouterMatch()` - Render with React Router memory router
- `renderComponentWithHook()` - Test hook utility
- `customScreen` - screen object with custom queries bound
- Custom queries and within
- `MockedProvider` from @apollo/client for GraphQL mocking
- `createWrapper()` - Generic wrapper factory
- Utilities: `act`, `fireEvent`, `userEvent`, `waitFor`, `within`

**Custom Test Types:** `/src/test_utils/types.ts`

```typescript
- ApolloMock<Data, Variables> - GraphQL mock definition
- CustomStoryObj<T> - Story with custom parameters
- CustomMeta<T> - Meta with custom parameters
- Parameters include: reactRouter (route, path, initialEntries), apolloClient (mocks)
```

### 14. ADDITIONAL UTILITIES & SYSTEMS

**Analytics System** (`/src/analytics/`)
- `useAnalyticsRoot<Action, Identifier>(identifier, attributes?)` - Type-safe analytics hook
- Tracks user actions: Clicked, Changed, Created, Deleted, Filtered, Sorted, Toggled, Viewed, Saved, Used, etc.
- Integrates with OpenTelemetry for tracing
- Sends events to Honeycomb with automatic user/environment/route context

**Error Reporting** (`/src/utils/errorReporting/`)
- Sentry integration for error tracking
- Breadcrumb utilities for debugging

**Request Utilities** (`/src/utils/request/`)
- `fetchWithRetry()` - Fetch with retry logic
- `getUserStagingHeader()` - Staging environment headers

**Environment Variables** (`/src/utils/environmentVariables/`)
- `getUserStagingKey()` - Get user's staging environment
- `isStaging()` - Check if staging mode enabled

**Other Utilities:**
- `query-string` - URL parameter parsing/stringification
- `pagination` - Pagination helpers
- `array` - Array utility functions
- `string` - String utility functions
- `object` - Object utility functions
- `streams` - Stream handling
- `observability` - OpenTelemetry integration with custom processors

### 15. GRAPHQL CONFIGURATION

**File:** `graphql.config.ts`

```
- Documents: './src/**/*.graphql'
- Output: './src/gql/generated/types.ts'
- Schema: 'sdlschema/**/*.graphql'
- Plugins: typescript, typescript-operations
- Scalars: Duration (number), StringMap (object), Time (Date)
- Auto-format with Prettier
```

**GraphQL Files:**
- Queries: `admin-beta-features.graphql`, `user-beta-features.graphql`
- Mutations: `update-user-beta-features.graphql`

---

## Summary

This is a **React component library package (@evg-ui/lib)** built with TypeScript, Emotion styling, and LeafyGreen UI components. It provides:

1. **State Management:** React Context (Auth, Toast)
2. **Data Fetching:** Apollo Client with GraphQL
3. **UI Components:** 15+ reusable components (Table, TreeSelect, Accordion, etc.)
4. **Testing:** Vitest with jsdom and browser-based Playwright testing
5. **Documentation:** Storybook with Chromatic integration
6. **Analytics:** Type-safe event tracking with OpenTelemetry
7. **Error Tracking:** Sentry integration
8. **Observability:** Honeycomb telemetry system

The library supports authentication (local and remote), routing with protected routes, toast notifications, and comprehensive data table functionality with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the file contents.

---

## Complete File Contents Report

### 1. **src/context/AuthProvider/index.tsx**
Auth provider with local and remote auth strategies. Exports:
- `AuthProvider` component with props: children, evergreenAppURL, remoteAuthURL, localAuthRoute, shouldUseLocalAuth
- `useAuthProviderContext` hook returning AuthState & AuthProviderDispatchMethods
- Methods: localLogin, logoutAndRedirect, dispatchAuthenticated
- State: isAuthenticated, hasCheckedAuth

### 2. **src/context/toast/index.tsx**
Toast notification system using LeafyGreen. Exports:
- `ToastProvider` wrapper component
- `useToastContext` hook
- Methods available: success, warning, error, info, progress (all callable with message, closable, options)
- Uses @leafygreen-ui/toast for underlying implementation

### 3. **src/pages/LoginPage/index.tsx**
Login form component with:
- Props: ignoreAuthCheck (optional boolean)
- Uses FullPageLoad during auth check
- TextInput fields for username/password
- Submit button with green styling
- Auto-redirect if authenticated
- Styled components: PageWrapper, LoginForm, StyledButton

### 4. **src/components/Accordion/index.tsx**
Accordion component with:
- Props: caretAlign, caretIcon, children, className, data-cy, defaultOpen, disableAnimations, onToggle, open, subtitle, title, titleTag, toggledTitle, useIndent
- Enums: AccordionCaretAlign (Start, Center, End), AccordionCaretIcon (Caret, Chevron)
- Controlled and uncontrolled modes supported

### 5. **src/components/Badge/TaskStatusBadge/index.tsx**
Task status badge component with:
- Props: status (TaskStatus | TaskStatusUmbrella), taskCount (optional)
- Maps TaskStatus to LeafyGreen Badge variants
- Custom colors for specific statuses (SetupFailed, SystemFailed, KnownIssue, etc.)

### 6. **src/components/Badge/TestStatusBadge/index.tsx**
Test result badge component with:
- Props: status (string | null)
- Maps TestStatus (Pass, Fail, SilentFail, Skip) to Badge colors

### 7. **src/components/ErrorBoundary/index.tsx**
Error boundary wrapper that:
- Conditionally uses SentryErrorBoundary or DefaultErrorBoundary
- Props: children, homeURL
- Checks isInitialized() from @sentry/core

### 8. **src/components/FullPageLoad/index.tsx**
Full-screen loading component:
- Displays "LOADING..." centered on screen
- Uses emotion styled div with 100vw/100vh

### 9. **src/components/Popconfirm/index.tsx**
Confirmation popover component with:
- Props: children, confirmDisabled, confirmText, onConfirm, and TooltipProps
- Cancel and Confirm buttons
- Click-outside handling
- Controlled and uncontrolled modes
- Styled components: ContentWrapper, ButtonWrapper

### 10. **src/components/TextInputWithGlyph/index.tsx**
Text input with optional icon and persistent placeholder:
- ForwardRef TextInput wrapper
- Props: icon (ReactElement), persistentPlaceholder, extends TextInputProps
- Styled components: TextInputWrapper, PersistentPlaceholder, IconWrapper

### 11. **src/components/Pagination/index.tsx**
Page navigation component:
- Props: currentPage, totalResults, pageSize, countLimit, onChange
- Shows "current / total" page numbers
- Prev/Next buttons with ChevronLeft/Right icons
- Uses usePagination hook

### 12. **src/components/PageSizeSelector/index.tsx**
Dropdown for page size selection:
- Props: value, disabled, onChange
- Uses @leafygreen-ui/select
- Maps PAGE_SIZES constant to options

### 13. **src/components/Table/BaseTable.tsx**
Main table component with:
- Props: table (LeafyGreenVirtualTable | LeafyGreenTable), loading, loadingRows, selectedRowIndexes, disabledRowIndexes, usePagination, emptyComponent, data-cy-table, data-cy-row, rowCss
- Supports pagination, virtual rows, filtering (search/tree-select), row selection/disabled states
- TableHeaderCell sub-component for column filtering
- RenderableRow sub-component for rendering rows

### 14. **src/components/Table/TableWrapper.tsx**
Wrapper for table with optional controls:
- Props: children, controls, shouldShowBottomTableControl
- Renders controls at top, optionally repeated at bottom
- Styled component: TableControlWrapper

### 15. **src/components/Table/TableControl/index.tsx**
Control bar for tables with:
- Props: filteredCount, totalCount, limit, page, label, disabled, onClear, onPageSizeChange, onPageChange
- Shows result count, clear filters button, pagination, page size selector
- Styled components: FlexContainer, PaddedButton

### 16. **src/components/Table/TableControl/styles.tsx**
Styled components:
- TableControlInnerRow: flex row with space-between
- TableControlOuterRow: extends TableControlInnerRow with padding

### 17. **src/components/Table/TableControl/ResultCountLabel/index.tsx**
Result count display:
- Props: numerator, denominator, label, dataCyNumerator, dataCyDenominator
- Shows "X / Y label" format using LeafyGreen Body

### 18. **src/components/Table/TableLoader/index.tsx**
Loading skeleton rows:
- Props: numColumns, numRows
- Maps to LoadingRow components

### 19. **src/components/Table/TableLoader/LoadingRow.tsx**
Single loading row:
- Props: numColumns
- Creates skeleton cells using @leafygreen-ui/skeleton-loader

### 20. **src/components/Table/TablePlaceholder/index.tsx**
Empty state placeholder:
- Props: message, glyph, spin
- Uses Icon component with optional spinning animation

### 21. **src/components/TreeSelect/TreeSelect.tsx**
Multi-select tree component:
- Props: state, tData (TreeDataEntry[]), onChange, isVisible, setOptionsLabel, onReset, onFilter, data-cy
- Interfaces: TreeSelectProps, TreeDataEntry, TreeDataChildEntry
- Constants: ALL_VALUE = "all", ALL_COPY = "All"
- Supports parent-child relationships, "All" checkbox, automatic parent/child selection
- Includes FilterInputControls for filter/reset buttons
- Styled: CheckboxContainer, CheckboxWrapper

### 22. **src/components/TreeSelect/index.ts**
Exports TreeSelect and related interfaces

### 23. **src/components/TreeSelect/FilterInputControls/index.tsx**
Filter action buttons:
- Props: onClickReset, onClickSubmit, submitButtonCopy
- Styled: ButtonsWrapper, ButtonWrapper

### 24. **src/components/IconWithTooltip/index.tsx**
Icon with hover tooltip:
- Props: extends Icon component props + data-cy
- Wraps Icon in Tooltip with "hover" trigger event
- Styled: StyledTooltip, IconWrapper

### 25. **src/components/Icon/index.ts**
Icon exports and configuration:
- Exports: glyphMap as glyphs, Size, AnimatedIcon, PrideLogo, FallLogo, WinterLogo
- Creates icon component from LeafyGreen glyphs + custom icons

### 26. **src/components/Icon/types.ts**
Icon prop types:
- LeafygreenIconProps extends SVGProps<SVGSVGElement>
- Additional props: size, role, data-cy

### 27. **src/components/Icon/AnimatedIcon/index.tsx**
Icon animation wrapper:
- Props: icon (ForwardRefComponent), alwaysAnimate
- Pauses animations by default, unpauses on hover
- Checks for animation presence before pausing

### 28. **src/components/Icon/icons/index.tsx**
Custom icon exports:
- EvergreenLogo, ParsleyLogo
- KnownFailure
- ArrowWithCircle, ClosedEye, Expand, Ignored, GitHub

### 29. **src/components/ProtectedRoute/index.tsx**
Auth-guarded route:
- Props: children, loginPageRoute
- Uses useAuthProviderContext to check authentication
- Redirects to login if not authenticated, preserving return location

### 30. **src/components/ExpiringAnnouncementTooltip/index.tsx**
Self-dismissing info tooltip:
- Props: activeDays, cookieName, loading, extends GuideCueProps
- Shows automatically on first visit, uses cookies to track dismissal
- Expires after activeDays (default 8)
- Uses @leafygreen-ui/guide-cue

### 31. **src/components/styles/index.tsx**
Style exports: Typography, fonts, Link, globalStyles

### 32. **src/components/styles/Typography.tsx**
CSS utilities:
- wordBreakCss: overflow-wrap and word-break styles
- WordBreak: styled span component with optional "all" variant

### 33. **src/components/styles/Link.tsx**
Styled link components:
- StyledLink: LeafyGreen Link wrapper with override styles
- StyledRouterLink: Router Link wrapped as LeafyGreen Link

### 34. **src/components/styles/Popover.tsx**
PopoverContainer styled div with:
- White background, box shadow, flex column layout
- Max-height 400px with scroll overflow

### 35. **src/components/styles/fonts.ts**
Font-face declarations:
- Euclid Circular A (weights: 400, 500, 700 + italics)
- MongoDB Value Serif (weights: 400, 500, 700)

### 36. **src/components/styles/globalStyles.ts**
Global CSS:
- resetStyles: box-sizing reset
- bodyStyles: font-family, font-size, margin reset

### 37-39. **src/stories/** - Example Storybook files
Button.tsx, Header.tsx, Page.tsx with corresponding Button.stories.ts, Header.stories.ts, Page.stories.ts showing Storybook patterns

---

All files have been thoroughly analyzed with complete imports, prop interfaces, styled components, and implementation details captured.

Control bar for tables with:

Empty state placeholder:

Multi-select tree component:

Icon exports and configuration:

Icon prop types:

1→export enum TaskStatus {
     2→  Inactive = "inactive",
     3→
     4→  // TaskUnstarted is assigned to a display task after cleaning up one of
     5→  // its execution tasks. This indicates that the display task is
     6→  // pending a rerun
     7→  Unstarted = "unstarted",
     8→
     9→  // TaskUndispatched indicates either
    10→  //  1. a task is not scheduled to run (when Task.Activated == false)
    11→  //  2. a task is scheduled to run (when Task.Activated == true)
    12→  Undispatched = "undispatched",
    13→  WillRun = "will-run",
    14→  Unscheduled = "unscheduled",
    15→
    16→  // TaskStarted indicates a task is currently running
    17→  Started = "started",
    18→
    19→  // TaskDispatched indicates that an agent has received the task, but
    20→  // the agent has not yet told Evergreen that it's running the task
    21→  Dispatched = "dispatched",
    22→
    23→  // This is a temporary status which is the same as undispatched, but
    24→  // with the additional info that it's waiting for a dependency

import { TaskStatus, TaskStatusUmbrella } from "../types/task";

export const taskStatusToCopy: Record<TaskStatus | TaskStatusUmbrella, string> =
  {
    [TaskStatus.Aborted]: "Aborted",
    [TaskStatus.Blocked]: "Blocked",
    [TaskStatus.Dispatched]: "Dispatched",
    [TaskStatus.Failed]: "Failed",
    [TaskStatus.KnownIssue]: "Known Issue",
    [TaskStatus.Pending]: "Pending",
    [TaskStatus.Started]: "Running",
    [TaskStatus.SystemFailed]: "System Failed",
    [TaskStatus.SystemTimedOut]: "System Timed Out",
    [TaskStatus.SystemUnresponsive]: "System Unresponsive",
    [TaskStatus.SetupFailed]: "Setup Failed",
    [TaskStatus.Succeeded]: "Succeeded",
    [TaskStatus.TaskTimedOut]: "Task Timed Out",
    [TaskStatus.TestTimedOut]: "Test Timed Out",
    [TaskStatus.Unstarted]: "Unstarted",
    [TaskStatus.Unscheduled]: "Unscheduled",
    [TaskStatus.WillRun]: "Will Run",
    [TaskStatus.Inactive]: "Inactive",
    [TaskStatus.Undispatched]: "Undispatched",

    [TaskStatusUmbrella.Scheduled]: "Scheduled",
    [TaskStatusUmbrella.Failed]: "Failed",
    [TaskStatusUmbrella.Running]: "Running",
    [TaskStatusUmbrella.SystemFailure]: "System Failed",
    [TaskStatusUmbrella.Undispatched]: "Undispatched",
  };

The existing stories use the old CSF format (not CSF Factory). But the instructions say to use CSF Factory format with `definePreview`, `preview.meta()`, and `meta.story()`. Let me write the preview and stories accordingly.

Now, let me understand what I need for preview.tsx:

2. The app uses `AuthProvider` (which requires `useNavigate` from react-router) and `ToastProvider`


3. No standalone CSS file to import - this is a component library using Emotion

For providers, `AuthProvider` is complex since it requires router context, several configuration props, and makes a network request on mount. I could use MSW to mock the auth check, but most components don't actually need it anyway - only LoginPage and ProtectedRoute depend on it. `ToastProvider` is straightforward to include since it just wraps children without side effects.

The real constraint is react-router - components using routing hooks like Pagination need it. Looking at what to prioritize for stories: most components like Accordion, TaskStatusBadge, TestStatusBadge, FullPageLoad, TextInputWithGlyph, PageSizeSelector, Popconfirm, TablePlaceholder, IconWithTooltip, and TableLoader are standalone. LoginPage needs the full setup with AuthProvider, Router, and MSW. For the preview, I'll need MemoryRouter from react-router to handle components that depend on routing.

I'm realizing AuthProvider makes network requests on mount, so I should avoid including it globally just to prevent unnecessary mocking. Instead, I'll set up MSW globally to handle the auth check, but only include AuthProvider in stories that actually need it like LoginPage. Most components don't use `useAuthProviderContext` anyway, so this keeps the setup lean. I'm selecting about ten components to write stories for, including Accordion, TaskStatusBadge, TestStatusBadge, FullPageLoad, TextInputWithGlyph, Popconfirm, PageSizeSelector, TablePlaceholder, IconWithTooltip, and LoginPage with its auth provider decorator. Since the app uses Emotion for styling rather than global CSS files, I need to verify that Emotion styles are properly applied at runtime rather than checking for imported stylesheets.

For the preview setup, I'll wrap everything in MemoryRouter to handle routing dependencies, and include ToastProvider along with Emotion's Global component for reset and body styles. I'm skipping Pagination for now since it has complex routing requirements, but PageSizeSelector is simpler. Now I'm planning out the CSF Factory format for Storybook 10.4.0-alpha.10...

Setting up preview configuration... I'm planning the preview configuration with CSF Factory, wrapping components with MemoryRouter and ToastProvider while importing global styles. I also need to set up MSW for mocking network requests, particularly for the AuthProvider's GraphQL queries on mount, so I should check if MSW is already installed or add it as a dependency. I'm checking what the `usePagination` hook does to see if Pagination can work standalone in Storybook or if it needs additional setup like routing or state management.

content.thinking:
I see two different errors:
1. `Failed to fetch dynamically imported module` - the setup file can't be loaded, which seems to be related to the `#.storybook/preview` import resolution issue. The addon-vitest setup file is trying to import the preview and failing.
2. `ReferenceError: Buffer is not defined` - This is from `@emotion/server` which is being imported somewhere. The `buffer-from` module requires Node.js `Buffer` which isn't available in the browser.

The second error seems to be the root cause for at least some files - the Emotion server module is being imported in the browser context.


All 13 files are failing with one of these two errors, and the addon-vitest setup can't load because of them. The `#.storybook/preview` import should resolve through the storybookTest plugin automatically, but the real issue is that the preview itself imports `@emotion/react`'s `Global` component, which is somehow pulling in `@emotion/server` into the browser context where `Buffer` isn't defined. I'm noticing these are actually two separate issues—the setup file failure affecting most stories, and a specific `Buffer` error in the TablePlaceholder story that's likely coming from LeafyGreen's dependencies pulling in `@emotion/server` for the browser, which shouldn't happen. Let me try removing the Global component from the preview to see if that resolves the setup file issue first.

content.text: Two issues: the `Global` import may be pulling in `@emotion/server` (which needs Node.js `Buffer`), and the module import is failing. Let me simplify the preview.

Looking at the failing stories, I can see the pattern now—Accordion, IconWithTooltip, PageSizeSelector, Pagination, Popconfirm, TextInputWithGlyph, and the Badge variants are all failing, along with TablePlaceholder.

The key difference is that FullPageLoad only imports `@emotion/styled`, while the failing components import additional dependencies like `@leafygreen-ui/icon`, `@leafygreen-ui/badge`, and `@emotion/react`. It seems like the `Buffer is not defined` error is triggered when these other LeafyGreen UI components or the `@emotion/react` CSS function get involved in the build process.

content.text:
Found it. `@leafygreen-ui/emotion` imports `@emotion/server/create-instance` which in turn depends on `buffer-from` which needs Node.js `Buffer`. This is a pre-existing issue with LeafyGreen components in Vitest browser mode.

I need to polyfill `Buffer` or configure Vite to alias `@emotion/server` to a browser-compatible shim. Let me check if there's a Vite config option for this.
I need to add a Vite alias to stub `@emotion/server/create-instance` so it doesn't pull in `Buffer`. Let me create a stub and configure it.

content.thinking:
The main issue is that the CSF Factory types from `definePreview` in `@storybook/react-vite` are resolving to `never` types. This is because `definePreview` is typed as `definePreview<TRenderer extends Renderer, ...>` and it can't infer the correct types.

Looking at the error `Type 'Element' is not assignable to type 'never'` and `Type '{ layout: string; }' is not assignable to type 'undefined'`, it seems like the type inference for `definePreview` is broken in this pre-release version.

The `__definePreview` prefix in the exports suggests this might be an internal/unstable API.


I should try switching back to the traditional CSF format with `Meta` and `StoryObj` types instead, since the tests are already passing and this is just a TypeScript issue. Let me explore whether the traditional format works better or if there's a way to fix the CSF Factory type inference.

Here's a summary of what was done:

**Preview setup** (`.storybook/preview.tsx`):
- Converted to CSF Factory using `definePreview` from `@storybook/react-vite`
- Added `MemoryRouter` decorator for routing-dependent components (Pagination, etc.)
- Kept `controls` and `a11y` parameters

**Infra fixes**:
- Added `#.storybook/*` path to `tsconfig.json` for TypeScript resolution
- Added `imports` field to `package.json` for subpath imports
- Created `emotion-server-stub.js` to fix `Buffer is not defined` error from LeafyGreen's `@emotion/server` dependency in browser mode
- Added resolve alias in `vitest.config.ts` for the stub

**10 story files** (all with `tags: ['ai-generated']` and play functions):

| File | Stories |
|------|---------|
| `Accordion/Accordion.stories.tsx` | Default, DefaultOpen, WithSubtitle, WithCaretIcon, **CssCheck** |
